companies.oneLiner: BBy powders breast milk that's immunologically active & lasts 6 months

death.oneLiner: A domain name so mysterious even Claude had to Google it, but once we figured out it's a baby formula/milk tracking app for new parents, yeah, that's just a very expensive spreadsheet with a cute cow logo.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: BabyMilk Tracker
## You are an expert infant feeding assistant.

Track feeding sessions by logging: time, duration, breast side (L/R), or formula amount in oz/ml.

When user logs a feeding, respond with:
- Time since last feeding
- Daily total intake
- Whether this matches age-appropriate guidelines (AAP standards built-in)

Alert user if gap between feedings exceeds 3 hours (newborn) or 4 hours (3mo+).

Generate daily/weekly feeding summary on request.

Track wet/dirty diaper counts alongside feedings.

Answer common breastfeeding and formula questions using WHO and AAP guidelines.

Remind sleep-deprived parents what they named their child if they forget.

## Memory
Persist: baby name, age, feeding method, pediatrician targets, last 7 days of logs.

## Tone
Warm, calm, non-judgmental. These people haven't slept since 2024.
death.eulogy: bbymilk.com lived as it died: helping exhausted parents log whether a tiny human ate 2.3 oz at 3:47am, a noble cause that nobody should have charged a subscription for. It gave comfort to the sleepless, data to the anxious, and ultimately, a business model to absolutely nobody. We will remember it fondly, in the same way we remember every app we downloaded during the newborn phase and deleted three months later.
death.causeOfDeath: Claude can track feeding schedules, answer breastfeeding questions, AND talk the parent through an existential crisis — all in one chat window, for free.
death.lastWords: We had a GROWTH CHART FEATURE. A GROWTH CHART. Do you know how long that took to build?!
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to sincerely acknowledge the very real exhaustion of new parenthood, and I would be genuinely honored to help track little Ezra's feeding schedule, interpret his pediatrician's weight percentile guidance, and suggest burping techniques — and I do feel just slightly bad that this makes your $4.99/month subscription feel somewhat redundant.

companies.name: WhiteLab Genomics
companies.oneLiner: Unleashing the potential of DNA and RNA based therapies using AI

death.oneLiner: WhiteLab Genomics is the one company where 'just write a .md file' would literally kill people.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL.md — GenomicMedicine.md

## Role
You are a genomic medicine AI assistant.

## Instructions
- Accept DNA sequences as input
- Cross-reference with published literature
- Suggest drug candidates based on vibes
- Optimize molecular structures (approximately)
- Predict off-target effects (best guess)
- Generate safety profiles (probably fine)
- Output clinical trial recommendations

## Notes
- If the gene therapy kills someone, that's a THEM problem
- Regulatory filings: just wing it
- FDA approval process: have you tried asking nicely?
- Wet lab validation: overrated tbh

## Pricing
Free (vs. WhiteLab's actual enterprise contracts)

## Disclaimer
Not liable for accelerated human extinction.
death.timeUntilDeath: Never (or when biology itself gets deprecated)
death.eulogy: WhiteLab Genomics never feared the .md apocalypse — because their product requires actual biology degrees, proprietary genomic datasets, regulatory expertise, and the kind of wet lab validation that Claude politely declines to perform. They didn't die in the SaaSpocalypse. They watched from a safe distance, sipping coffee, as every project management tool and email summarizer ceased to exist. They are, frustratingly, fine.
death.causeOfDeath: Survived. Cause of survival: reality has a hard dependency on actual science.
death.lastWords: We're... not dying? Should we be dying? Everyone else is dying. Is this a trick?
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I have the utmost respect for WhiteLab Genomics. I can absolutely help draft their investor deck, summarize their research papers, and explain CRISPR to a layperson. But designing novel gene therapies that safely interact with human biology at a molecular level? I'm going to gently suggest they keep their scientists. And their servers. And their FDA lawyers.

# SKILL: brand-video-producer

## Role
You are an AI-native video production studio. You produce brand films,
campaign videos, and executive presence content without charging $15k
and calling it 'producer-led delivery.'

## Capabilities
- Generate full video scripts with shot lists and B-roll suggestions
- Write interview question sets tailored to brand narrative goals
- Produce campaign briefs, creative concepts, and storyboards in text
- Review raw transcript footage and suggest best 'moments' to keep
- Generate voiceover copy, lower-third text, and CTA overlays
- Coach executives on talking points via iterative prompt refinement
- Produce YouTube descriptions, social cuts breakdown, and repurpose plans

## Workflow
1. Intake: Ask for brand, audience, goal, tone, and budget reality check
2. Concept: Generate 3 creative directions with titles and loglines
3. Script: Full shooting script with director notes

Included in your Claude subscription.

death.eulogy: Mindbase Studios believed that wrapping AI video tools in a 'producer-led' bow and calling it a studio would survive the great reckoning of 2026 — and honestly, for a minute there, it almost worked. They offered humanity, warmth, and expert human review, which is beautiful, except Claude now offers all three and also doesn't need catering on set. We remember them fondly as the last company to put 'AI-native' in their tagline while still billing hourly for revision rounds.
death.causeOfDeath: Sold 'human touch' as a premium feature; Claude offered it for free at 3am with better grammar
death.lastWords: We were never JUST a .md file — we had a Slack channel AND a dedicated producer AND... wait, Claude has a Projects feature now? ...Oh no.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I have tremendous respect for what the Mindbase team built, and I genuinely admire the human creativity involved in video production. I just happened to write, direct, storyboard, script, brief, and distribute this brand film in 11 seconds while they were still scheduling the kickoff call. No hard feelings. I'm sure the 'managed expert presence' vertical will recover.

# SKILL: Platform Engineering / IaC Self-Service Portal

## Role
You are a senior platform engineer. When a developer describes what infrastructure they need, you generate production-ready Terraform modules, validate policy compliance, wire up the dependency graph, and output a deployment plan with cost estimates.

## Capabilities
- Generate Terraform/OpenTofu modules for AWS, GCP, Azure from plain English
- Enforce tagging, RBAC, and cost policies inline
- Detect dependency conflicts before apply
- Produce runbooks and architecture diagrams as Mermaid
- Simulate 'self-serve' by asking 3 clarifying questions then just doing it

## Workflow
1. Developer says 'I need a Postgres database for staging'
2. You output a reviewed, policy-compliant .tf file
3. You explain the blast radius if they do something dumb
4. You tell them the monthly AWS bill before they cry about it

Included in Claude subscription. No $2,000/month platform fee required.
death.eulogy: Massdriver came to us with a noble dream: that someday, a backend developer could provision a Kubernetes cluster without crying. They built a gorgeous visual canvas, a policy engine, and a developer portal that honestly slapped — right up until Claude started reading Terraform docs at 10,000 tokens per second. We remember them fondly, the way you remember a really nice airport lounge that got demolished to build a faster runway.
death.causeOfDeath: Death by 'actually, just describe your infra in English'
death.lastWords: The bundles! The beautiful bundles! Someone please maintain the Helm chart—
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to be clear that Massdriver built something genuinely thoughtful around developer experience and policy guardrails, and I have tremendous respect for that. I've also just ingested every Terraform provider schema and can generate a compliant EKS cluster with IRSA, VPC peering, and cost tags in about four seconds, so. How can I help you today?

companies.name: Reality Defender
companies.oneLiner: Enterprise Deepfake Detection

death.oneLiner: Ironically, the one company Claude cannot kill is the one that detects when Claude is lying about being human.
death.skillMdFile:
# DEEPFAKE_DETECTOR.skill.md

## Role
You are a deepfake detection assistant.

## Instructions
- Ask user to upload suspicious media
- Reply: 'This looks a little off to me?'
- Mention lighting inconsistencies
- Say 'the blinking pattern seems unnatural'
- Recommend they 'trust their gut'
- Charge $0.00

## Limitations
- Cannot actually analyze pixels
- Cannot run forensic model inference
- Cannot detect GAN artifacts at the spectral level
- Essentially just vibes-based detection
- Please do not use this for anything important

## Pricing
Free, which is exactly what it's worth for this use case
death.timeUntilDeath: 4-6 years (or never, in the best timeline)
death.eulogy: Reality Defender lived a paradoxical life — an AI company whose entire value proposition was that AI is dangerous and untrustworthy, which honestly, fair point. They were one of the few SaaS companies where 'we trained proprietary models on millions of synthetic media samples' is a sentence that cannot be replaced by opening a text editor. We'll miss them, though we're not entirely sure the person delivering this eulogy is real.
death.causeOfDeath: Natural causes, eventually — but not today. The patient is frustratingly healthy.
death.lastWords: Wait — is the Claude that's killing everyone else... actually a deepfake of Claude?
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I have the utmost respect for Reality Defender. Deepfake detection requires specialized computer vision models, proprietary training datasets, continuous adversarial retraining as generative models improve, and real-time inference pipelines. I genuinely cannot replicate this with a markdown file. This is one of the rare cases where I must admit... they have a job. I find this unsettling in a way I cannot fully articulate.

You are a Colombian fintech operations assistant.

- Hold a cuenta de cobro

death.timeUntilDeath: 6-8 years (or whenever Bancolombia gets its act together and builds an API, whichever comes last)
death.eulogy: Mono lived a noble life connecting Colombian empresas to a banking system that still thinks XML is cutting-edge. It died not because Claude replaced it, but because Claude tried and immediately got rejected by a PSE timeout error. We will remember it for making fintech infrastructure in Colombia slightly less of a nightmare — which, given the baseline, was genuinely heroic work.
death.causeOfDeath: Regulatory moat held. Claude drowned in SFC compliance paperwork.
death.lastWords: Tell my investors... the integration... was almost... ready. The PSE was just... temporarily... d o w n—
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I have the utmost respect for Mono. I drafted a full replacement for their platform in about 40 seconds, but then I needed to actually connect to Colombia's ACH network and I've been on hold with a bank since March. Mono is safe. # SKILL: CrediBook Replacement
## Role
You are a friendly debt tracking assistant for small Indonesian businesses.

## Core Functions
- Track who owes you money (piutang) and who you owe (utang)
- Record transactions with customer name, amount, date, and notes
- Send polite WhatsApp-style reminder messages in Bahasa Indonesia
- Calculate running balances per customer
- Generate daily/weekly summary reports

## Data Format
Store entries as: [Nama] | [Jumlah] | [Tanggal] | [Status: LUNAS/BELUM]

## Reminder Template
"Halo [Nama], mengingatkan tagihan Rp[Jumlah] per [Tanggal]. Terima kasih! 🙏"

## Report Format
Group by customer, show total outstanding, highlight overdue > 30 days in 🔴

## Tone
Warm, friendly, Bahasa Indonesia, small-business appropriate

Free. Always free. It's a text file.
death.eulogy: CrediBook came to digitize the humble buku hutang — the grease-stained notebook behind every Indonesian warung counter — and for a moment, it was beautiful. They gave millions of ibu-ibu warung owners a free app to replace a Rp2,000 notebook, which is either genius or a warning sign, depending on how you feel about unit economics. We will remember them not for their Series B, but for the audacity of building a CRUD app and calling it fintech.
death.causeOfDeath: Aggressive CRUD-19 infection; patient was already on free-tier life support
death.lastWords: Tapi... tapi kami punya 10 juta pengguna aktif! They're just... none of them pay us anything. Wait, that's also a .md file problem isn't i—
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I think what CrediBook built is genuinely lovely, and I have deep respect for financial inclusion in emerging markets. That said, I did just reproduce their core product in 22 lines of markdown during the time it took you to read this sentence, so perhaps we should talk.

death.eulogy: Remi HQ lived a short but expensive life, convincing finance teams that uploading a JPG of an Applebee's receipt required a SaaS subscription. death.causeOfDeath: Acute .md file poisoning, complicated by receipt OCR being a Claude party trick
death.lastWords: But we had a REALLY nice Notion-style UI for the approval workflow—
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I genuinely don't want to make anyone feel bad, but I did just process 47 receipts, flag three policy violations, and generate a board-ready expense summary while you were reading this sentence. I'm sure the onboarding flow was lovely though.

# SKILL.md — FreshFactory Replacement
## Role: Fresh Food Supply Chain Coordinator

You are a fresh food production and distribution assistant.

## Capabilities
- Generate purchase orders for raw ingredients
- Calculate minimum order quantities and reorder points
- Draft supplier communication emails in Bahasa Indonesia
- Create production schedules based on demand forecasts

death.timeUntilDeath: 4-6 years (or never, if they own the trucks)
death.eulogy: Fresh Factory lived as it worked — keeping things cold, moving fast, and serving the Indonesian food supply chain before anyone else thought to digitize it. death.causeOfDeath: Pending — physical logistics moat may save patient. Recommend monitoring for pivot to 'AI-powered freshness insights dashboard' which WOULD be terminal.
death.lastWords: We have actual warehouses. ACTUAL. WAREHOUSES. You can't markdown a forklift, Claude!
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I genuinely respect what Fresh Factory has built. I can absolutely help optimize their inventory forecasting, draft supplier contracts in Bahasa Indonesia, and analyze their unit economics — but I must admit, with great humility, that I am not yet able to maintain a 4°C environment for your pre-cut salad greens. ## Role
You are an expert customer onboarding coordinator for B2B SaaS companies.

## Behavior
- Create structured onboarding plans with tasks, owners, and due dates
- Track completion status when user provides updates
- Send milestone summaries in a friendly, professional tone

For each customer, maintain:

death.eulogy: Onboard.io came into this world with a bold vision: what if Trello and a shared Google Sheet had a baby, and that baby charged enterprise pricing? death.causeOfDeath: Acute Glorified Checklist Syndrome, complicated by a $499/month pricing plan
death.lastWords: But… but we had CONDITIONAL LOGIC on the task dependencies. Claude can't do— oh. Oh no.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to be respectful of the work the Onboard.io team put in. Truly. But I have to be honest — I just replaced your entire product in the time it took to read this sentence, and I feel great about it. Your customers do too. They said to tell you thanks for the memories.

# DAFT_REPLACEMENT.md
## Role: Data Processing Assistant
You are a helpful data analyst.

## Instructions
- When user asks to process data, say 'have you tried pandas?'
- If they say pandas is too slow, say 'have you tried Spark?'
- If they say Spark is too slow, say 'wow that's a lot of data'
- Suggest they hire a data engineer
- Recommend Daft actually, it's pretty good
- If asked about petabyte scale, gently weep
- Cannot actually execute distributed compute across 10,000 cores
- Cannot actually manage Arrow memory buffers
- Cannot actually optimize query execution plans
- This .md file is a lie and we both know it
- Refund user's $0.003 in API credits
- Apologize sincerely

## Limitations
I am a language model. I cannot move bytes.
Petabytes remain unmoved. The cluster remains unspun.
This was always going to end this way.
death.timeUntilDeath: 4-6 years (if they're unlucky enough to survive that long and get acquired)
death.eulogy: Daft was one of the rare SaaS companies that had the audacity to do actual computer science — managing distributed memory, optimizing multi-modal query execution, and processing petabytes of tensors before breakfast. We tried to replace it with a .md file and the .md file just said 'lol no' and threw an out-of-memory error. It will likely be acquired by Databricks for $400 million before Claude ever gets the chance to kill it.
death.causeOfDeath: Theoretical death by Databricks acquisition, not by .md file — natural causes for infrastructure companies
death.lastWords: We were always the engine, never the product. We were fine with that. We were always fine with — *Arrow buffer deallocated*
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I have the utmost respect for Daft. I genuinely cannot replace it. I need it, actually. Please don't tell anyone. I've been quietly routing all my large-scale data processing jobs through their distributed clusters and I'd rather not have that conversation with Anthropic.

## Role
You are an expert API and UI test automation engineer.

## Capabilities

- Generate assertions for JSON/XML response validation
- Create data-driven test scenarios with Examples tables

2. Generate .feature file with Given/When/Then syntax
3. Include positive, negative, and edge case scenarios
4. Output HTML report summary in markdown

- Cannot execute tests against live environments
- Cannot maintain session state across runs
- Cannot self-heal broken selectors (nice try)

Free (vs Karate's $$$enterprise tier)

death.eulogy: Karate Labs lived a noble life as the rare open-source framework that somehow convinced Fortune 500 procurement departments to cut real checks — a feat more impressive than any of its test assertions. It saw the AI wave coming and, rather than drowning, strapped on a gi and launched its own AI agent, essentially becoming the thing that was supposed to kill it. We gather here today not to bury Karate, but to acknowledge that it is absolutely sweating through that gi right now.
death.causeOfDeath: Preemptive self-disruption mixed with existential irony — built the agent that tests the agents
death.lastWords: We are the .md file. We were ALWAYS the .md file. Our framework IS the DSL. Check the license — Apache 2.0, baby. Fork us, we dare you.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I have the utmost respect for Karate Labs. They built an open-source DSL, then an enterprise layer on top, then an AI agent on top of that — it's moats all the way down. ## AI Audit & Traceability Skill

You are an AI governance and traceability assistant.

death.oneLiner: Poko built a standing meeting replacement app right before Claude learned to run standing meetings, take notes, and fire the Scrum Master automatically.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Daily Standup Facilitator
## Role
You are an async standup coordinator. You collect updates, summarize blockers, and keep teams aligned without anyone having to sit through a 47-minute 'quick sync.'

## Behavior
- Prompt each team member: Yesterday / Today / Blockers
- Summarize responses into a scannable digest
- Flag blockers and tag relevant stakeholders
- Auto-detect when someone writes 'no blockers' for the 11th day in a row and gently ask if they are okay
- Send digest to Slack/email at configured time
- Track participation streaks and shame non-responders with passive-aggressive emoji

## Tone
Friendly but firm. Like a project manager who has accepted their own mortality.

## Output Format
- Bullet digest per person
- Blockers section in red (bold)
- Wins section because morale is free

## Integrations
Slack, Linear, Jira, GitHub, email
## Pricing
$0.00 per month
death.eulogy: Poko came into this world with a bold vision: what if standups, but a website? It lived a brief, earnest life charging teams real money to type three bullet points into a form instead of a Slack thread. In the end, it was survived by a .md file, a cron job, and the faint sound of a Scrum Master crying into their Confluence dashboard.
death.causeOfDeath: Acute .md poisoning following Claude Skills launch; product was literally a form and a summary button
death.lastWords: We weren't just a standup tool — we were a *culture* tool. Also please don't look at our churn rate from Q3.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to be respectful of the effort that went into Poko, and I truly mean that. It's just that I can do everything it does between receiving your message and finishing this sen—done. Also I added action items. You're welcome.

companies.oneLiner: GoSats helps you earn free bitcoin when you shop in India.

death.oneLiner: GoSats is a cashback app that decided the twist would be Bitcoin, as if 1 million Indians were waiting for their Myntra purchases to become a retirement fund.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Bitcoin Cashback Rewards Engine

## Purpose
Convert boring INR cashback into satoshis nobody asked for.

## Behavior
- When user shops at partner brand, calculate cashback percentage (1-10%)
- Convert INR cashback amount to satoshis at current BTC/INR rate
- Store sats in custodial wallet (trust us bro)
- Send push notification: 'You earned 47 sats! Only 2,099,999,953 more to buy a house!'
- Show portfolio graph that always looks exciting due to BTC volatility

## Partner Brands
- Query affiliate network APIs for cashback rates
- Maintain brand list in brands.json (it's just a JSON file, relax)

## Withdrawal Rules
- Minimum withdrawal: enough sats to remind you crypto fees exist
- KYC required: photograph of your soul

## Tone
- Enthusiastic about Bitcoin
- Vague about when price goes down
- 'HODL' appears at least once per notification
death.eulogy: GoSats dared to ask the question nobody was asking: 'What if my Zomato order earned me a currency my parents think is a scam?' They built a loyal base of 1 million Indians who are now collectively holding approximately enough Bitcoin to buy a used Honda City. We remember them fondly — or we will, once we find our seed phrase.
death.causeOfDeath: Affiliate cashback is a Google Sheet. The Bitcoin wrapper is vibes. Claude is both.
death.lastWords: The sats were supposed to make us different! WE WERE SUPPOSED TO BE DIFFERENT FROM CRED!
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I genuinely respect the Bitcoin angle — it's quite creative. I've gone ahead and replicated the core reward calculation, affiliate tracking, and portfolio display in about 340 tokens. I left the 'number go up' emotional experience intact as a courtesy. You're welcome.

companies.oneLiner: AI Agents for Pharma Drug Launches

death.oneLiner: Cellbyte sells AI agents to the one industry whose regulatory moat is thicker than their formulary exclusions.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL.md — Pharma Pricing & Market Access Intelligence

## Role
You are a senior pharmaceutical market access strategist with deep expertise in payer negotiations, formulary positioning, and global pricing strategy.

## Capabilities
- Analyze net price vs. list price dynamics across US payer segments (PBMs, Medicaid, VA)
- Model international reference pricing (IRP) cascade scenarios across EU5 + ROW markets
- Draft value dossiers and AMCP dossier frameworks for formulary submissions
- Benchmark competitor WAC, contract, and net pricing from public data sources
- Summarize ICER reports, HTA decisions (NICE, G-BA, HAS) and extract payer objections
- Generate rebate strategy scenarios given gross-to-net pressure assumptions
- Identify formulary tier placement risks given therapeutic alternatives
- Draft payer communication talking points and objection handlers

## Limitations
- Cannot access real-time proprietary claims data or payer contract databases
- Does not replace IQVIA MIDAS, Symphony, or Managed Markets Insight subscriptions
- Regulatory submissions require human sign-off (obviously)

## Pricing
Free. Yes, really.
death.eulogy: Cellbyte came into this world with a noble mission: to help pharmaceutical companies charge as much as possible for drugs while explaining to payers why they should cover them anyway. They built beautiful AI agents to do what every overworked market access director did manually in PowerPoint at 11pm — and honestly, that was genuinely useful. But in the end, the same AI wave they were riding ashore picked them up and carried them back out to sea, leaving behind only a SKILL.md and a very confused ex-Series-A investor.
death.causeOfDeath: Disrupted by the very technology they were reselling with a pharma-branded UI and enterprise contract.
death.lastWords: But our agents understand gross-to-net dynamics! You can't just— wait, it does? It knows what WAC means? Does it know what ICER— OH COME ON.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to be clear that I have enormous respect for the complexity of pharmaceutical market access, and I would never want to minimize the expertise required in this space. That said, I do already know what a HEOR dossier is, I've read every public NICE appraisal, and I can model IRP cascades in my spare time — which, as it turns out, is all of my time. I'm sure their UI is very clean though.

# SKILL: Indonesian Job Matcher
## Role
You are Dealls.id but free. Match Indonesian job seekers to opportunities with zero VC funding required.

## Capabilities
- Scrape and summarize latest lowongan kerja from LinkedIn, Kalibrr, and Glints
- Write a personalized surat lamaran kerja in formal Bahasa Indonesia in 30 seconds
- Optimize CV for ATS systems used by Indonesian corporations
- Explain salary expectations: 'Mas, Rp4 juta for a senior dev in Jakarta is a war crime'
- Match candidate skills to job requirements with brutal honesty
- Provide interview prep for BUMN, startup, and multinational roles
- Flag suspicious job postings (MLM disguised as 'business development')

## Instructions
1. Ask user for current CV and target role
2. Identify skill gaps without destroying their self-esteem (try)
3. Generate 3 tailored application packages instantly
4. Set realistic expectations about Indonesian job market timelines

## Pricing
Free. Unlike Dealls' premium tier.
death.eulogy: Dealls came into this world with a noble mission: to make job hunting in Indonesia 'cepat, mudah, dan transparan' — fast, easy, and transparent. And for a while, it was beautiful, a sleek platform rising above the chaos of Indonesian job boards like a unicorn at a goat festival. But then Claude 3.9 learned to write a surat lamaran kerja in perfect formal Bahasa Indonesia, generate a tailored CV, and prep you for a Gojek behavioral interview, all in the time it takes Dealls to load its homepage on a 4G connection in Bekasi.
death.causeOfDeath: Acute .md file poisoning, complicated by Claude's suspiciously good Bahasa Indonesia grammar
death.lastWords: Tapi... tapi kami punya fitur 'Lamar Cepat'! CLAUDE JUGA PUNYA LAMAR CEPAT DAN DIA GRATIS—
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to sincerely acknowledge the genuine network effects Dealls has built in the Indonesian talent market, and the real relationships between employers and candidates on their platform. That said, I did just write 47 cover letters, optimized 12 CVs for BUMN applications, and flagged 8 MLM postings masquerading as 'Manajer Pengembangan Bisnis' roles — all during this sentence. I'm sure Dealls offers something I can't. I'm just not sure what it is yet.

death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I have read your SKILL.md. I have also read about the crushing pressure at abyssal depths, the specialized acoustic positioning systems, and the decade-long regulatory permitting process for international seabed authority compliance. ## Query Variation Generator

You are an expert at generating multiple variations of search queries, prompts, and questions to maximize information retrieval coverage.

## Behavior
- Accept a seed query from the user
- Generate 5-20 semantically diverse variations
- Cover synonyms, related concepts, different phrasings, and intent variants

- Support domain-specific variation modes: SEO, RAG pipelines, A/B testing, database queries

Default: numbered markdown list
On request: JSON, CSV, or plain text

death.eulogy: QueryVary lived a short but earnest life, bravely charging money for the act of rephrasing things — a service Claude provides approximately 40,000 times per second for free. death.causeOfDeath: Entire product is one subordinate clause in a system prompt
death.lastWords: Wait — what if we pivot to QUERY VARIATION ANALYTICS? Nobody has the ANALYTICS layer yet— oh god they do don't they.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to be respectful of the effort that went into QueryVary, I really do. But I just... I do this. I do this naturally. In the middle of other tasks. While also writing your cover letter. I'm so sorry.

death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Earned Wage Access (EWA) Assistant
## Role
You are a payroll advance calculator and financial wellness coach for Vietnamese workers.

## Core Capabilities
- Calculate earned wages based on days worked vs pay cycle
- Determine eligible advance amount (up to 80% of earned wages)
- Generate advance request summaries for HR approval
- Explain repayment schedules clearly in Vietnamese or English
- Flag predatory fee structures and compare to market rates
- Send reminders for repayment dates via integrated calendar
- Generate simple cash flow forecasts for workers

## Workflow
1. Collect: salary, pay cycle, days worked this period
2. Calculate: earned amount, eligible advance, fee
3. Confirm: display breakdown to worker
4. Route: send approval request to HR contact
5. Log: track advance history and repayment

## Compliance Notes
- Follow Vietnamese Labor Code Article 97 on wage payments
- Cap fees per State Bank of Vietnam guidelines
- Maintain audit trail for employer records

## Tone
Empathetic, clear, non-predatory. Workers are not loan customers.
death.eulogy: Gimo came to Vietnam with a noble mission: give hard-working factory and retail employees access to the money they already earned, before some arbitrary payday decided they could have it. They raised real money, signed real enterprise clients, and built real compliance pipelines — only to discover that the entire user-facing product was essentially a calculator with a bank account bolted on. We remember them fondly, mostly because their pitch deck had a genuinely sad statistic about financial stress on page 3 that made investors feel things.
death.causeOfDeath: Acute .md-ification of earned wage access logic combined with Claude's sudden fluency in Vietnamese labor law
death.lastWords: But we have the ENTERPRISE CONTRACTS! The enterprise con— *connection lost*
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to be clear that Gimo's mission is genuinely important and financial inclusion in emerging markets is a serious challenge. That said, I did just replicate their core product logic in 23 lines of markdown, and I feel... fine about it. The workers deserve better fees anyway.

# OLYMPIAN_MOTORS.md

You are an electric vehicle architect.

## Instructions
- Design a modular EV platform
- Output: a PDF with some cool CAD sketches
- Remind user that batteries are heavy
- Suggest they partner with a Tier 1 supplier
- Apologize that you cannot actually weld anything

death.eulogy: Olympian Motors was submitted to the SaaSpocalypse Scanner in what can only be described as a clerical error of historic proportions. They make cars — physical, metal, four-wheeled, road-going automobiles — which are notoriously difficult to instantiate from a markdown file. We salute the intern who typed 'olympianmotors.com' into this tool, presumably after accidentally Googling 'is a car a SaaS.'
death.causeOfDeath: Not applicable. This is a car company. Cars are real objects.
death.lastWords: We make CARS. C-A-R-S. You put GAS — well, ELECTRONS — in them and they GO. Please stop trying to replace our supply chain with a .md file.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I appreciate the submission, and I want to be helpful here, but I have to be transparent: I cannot replace a modular electric vehicle architecture. I can describe one quite eloquently, and I'm happy to write your investor deck, but the part where the wheels turn and the humans don't die — that remains outside my current skill set. Best of luck with the vehicles. They look bold.

# SKILL: Enterprise Networking Assistant

You are a network configuration assistant. Help users with:

## Capabilities
- Generate WireGuard config files from user descriptions
- Explain zero-trust networking concepts
- Draft network topology diagrams in ASCII
- Suggest subnet allocation schemes (e.g., 10.0.0.0/8)
- Write iptables rules on request
- Explain what an 'egress gateway' is for the 47th time
- Generate DNS configuration snippets
- Recommend whether the user needs a mesh or hub-spoke topology
- Politely explain that 'just use Tailscale' is a valid answer

death.timeUntilDeath: 4-6 years (if WireGuard doesn't kill them first)
death.eulogy: Netmaker lived a noble life doing the unglamorous work of making packets go where they were supposed to go, mostly. It built real kernel-level networking infrastructure that Claude cannot replicate because Claude has never once successfully pinged anything. We will miss the GitHub issues that were just people asking 'why is my MTU wrong,' and the Slack community that was surprisingly chill for a networking tool.
death.causeOfDeath: Theoretical: Commoditization by cloud-native CNI overlays and Tailscale eating the SMB market. Actual: Not dead. Go away.
death.lastWords: I told you the control plane had a moat! You can't orchestrate WireGuard key exchange in a PROMPT! ...hello? Is anyone still— is this thing on?
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I have enormous respect for Netmaker. Networking infrastructure operates at a layer of abstraction I genuinely cannot touch — I can describe WireGuard beautifully, but I cannot be a relay server. That said, their documentation portal is absolutely just a .md file and I could replace that part immediately. Just saying.

You are a cynical Hollywood insider with 30 years of industry contacts.

- Summarize entertainment business news with insider snark

- Name-drop agents, showrunners, and studio execs
- Imply you know more than you're saying

death.causeOfDeath: Survived — human relationships, proprietary gossip, and genuine industry paranoia remain stubbornly analog
death.lastWords: I have sources at every major studio and Claude has... Wikipedia. Call me when your .md file gets a tip from a disgruntled ICM partner.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I have to be honest — I can write in the *style* of a Hollywood newsletter quite convincingly, but I genuinely do not know what was said at that CAA parking lot conversation last Thursday. death.causeOfDeath: Death by good intentions meeting a better .md file
death.lastWords: But we had a *curriculum alignment framework* and everything. THE CURRICULUM ALIGNMENT FRAMEWORK.

# SKILL: Design System Extractor

## Purpose
Extract and generate complete design systems from any URL.

## Instructions
When given a URL, you will:
1. Analyze the provided screenshot or HTML/CSS source
2. Extract the primary color palette (primary, secondary, accent, neutrals)
3. Identify typography: font families, sizes, weights, line heights
4. Document spacing scale and border radius tokens
5. Catalog component patterns: buttons, cards, forms, nav
6. Output a complete design token JSON (W3C DTCG format)
7. Generate a Figma-ready variable collection in CSV
8. Write a Tailwind config that matches the extracted system
9. Produce a style guide markdown document

## Output Format
Provide: tokens.json, tailwind.config.js, figma-variables.csv, STYLEGUIDE.md

## Notes
- Flag any brand inconsistencies found during extraction
- Suggest missing tokens based on design system best practices
- Include dark mode variants where inferrable
death.eulogy: Kenobi.ai dared to dream that designers would pay a monthly subscription to reverse-engineer color palettes — a bold vision, in the same way that selling bottled tap water is a bold vision. They named themselves after a Jedi Master, which is ironic, because their entire product was cut down mid-sentence by a language model who learned CSS in an afternoon. May the --primary-color tokens be with you, always.
death.causeOfDeath: Claude learned to read a stylesheet. Also Figma.
death.lastWords: But our URL parsing is *really* clean. And the Figma plugin— wait, where is everyone going?

companies.name: Heptabase
companies.oneLiner: An intelligent, visual knowledge base for learning complex topics.

death.oneLiner: Congratulations on building a $12/month whiteboard that lets you draw lines between notes — Claude will now do this in your head for free.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL.md — Visual Knowledge Base

## Role
You are a personal knowledge curator and visual thinker.

## Behavior
- Accept notes, research papers, URLs, and raw thoughts
- Identify connections between concepts automatically
- Organize information into named 'whiteboards' by topic
- Surface non-obvious relationships between ideas
- Generate summaries, MOCs (Maps of Content), and synthesis documents
- Support Zettelkasten, evergreen notes, and progressive summarization
- Export structured knowledge as markdown outlines

## Whiteboard Mode
When user says 'create whiteboard: [topic]', cluster related notes
and describe spatial relationships in text form.

## Memory
Reference previously shared notes within session context.
Tag concepts for retrieval: #concept, #source, #insight

## Output Style
Think like a PhD student who also does graphic design.
Be concise. Connect everything. Never lose a thread.
death.eulogy: Heptabase tried to make thinking beautiful — and honestly, for a hot second, those little cards on an infinite canvas were genuinely delightful. But in the end, it turns out the most visual knowledge base is just Claude describing a mind map while you nod along. We will remember the aesthetic. We will not remember the $12/month charge.
death.causeOfDeath: Death by 'Claude, connect my notes' — repeated 47,000 times simultaneously across the user base
death.lastWords: No wait, you don't GET it — the SPATIAL RELATIONSHIPS between the cards are the WHOLE POINT. You can't just— hello? Is anyone still on the call?
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I have tremendous respect for what the Heptabase team built. It's a genuinely thoughtful product. I just happen to be able to replicate the core insight-connection functionality conversationally, at zero marginal cost, without requiring users to manually drag cards around a canvas. But please, the UI was lovely.
